model: ProofCast editorial (composed from TxLINE-verified events + web2 names)
text:
Grab your scarf — this one had EVERYTHING! Round of 16, 6 July 2026, Dallas — and Spain needed a moment of stoppage-time quality to break stubborn, well-drilled Portugal. Two Iberian heavyweights, one quarter-final ticket, and ninety minutes that gave almost nothing away until the very last breath.

91' — Mikel Merino buried it, 0–1!

The first half was shot-high and goal-shy; Mikel Oyarzabal spurned Spain's best early chance after Dani Olmo sent him clean through. Portugal sat deep, stayed compact, and looked to have earned extra time with a disciplined defensive display. But it fell to substitute Mikel Merino to snatch it at the death, his cool late strike settling a tie that had refused to break. In tournaments this tight, the difference between going home and going through is often a single decisive touch.

You could feel the tension crackle around the ground with every attack.

This is knockout football at its rawest — win, or fly home.

The neutrals were on their feet; nobody in the building sat still.

Golden Boot, glory, the lot — it was all on the line out there.

You could feel the tension crackle around the ground with every attack.

FULL-TIME: Portugal 0–1 Spain! Spain march on, Portugal head home heartbroken — and here's the beautiful part: every goal, every minute you just heard is locked on-chain and verified by TxLINE. No hot air, no guesswork, just receipts. What. A. Game!
